arts.ttl: Crystal Palace position themselves to sign 21-year-old – Eagles have to overcome competition in summer
arts.sum: Crystal Palace have identified Royal Charleroi SC’s Cheick Keita as a transfer target for the summer window. That’s according to Africa Foot, who say Crystal Palace are one of the clubs to have shown interest in the centre-back. arts.ttl: Player ‘tempted’ by Aston Villa – Villans can sign him as club ‘waiting for offers’
arts.sum: Aston Villa were serious about signing Villarreal’s Juan Foyth in the winter market. The defender initially joined the La Liga side on loan from Tottenham Hotspur in 2020 and signed for them permanently a year later. This happened when Unai Emery was in charge at the club. arts.ttl: Preparations in case Aston Villa, Arsenal or Tottenham swoop in to sign player
arts.sum: Two weeks ago, Arsenal, Tottenham and Aston Villa were linked with a move for Fiorentina’s Moise Kean. The striker has scored 19 times in all competitions this season and this is already the best individual campaign of the Italian’s career. Performances like that are likely to attract the attention of clubs across Europe. arts.ttl: Crystal Palace vs Aston Villa – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Crystal Palace welcome Aston Villa to Selhurst Park in the Premier League this evening. Oliver Glasner’s side have won four of their last six league games after an impressive 2-0 win at Fulham at the weekend. That victory was a fourth straight win on the road for the Eagles, who will hope to match that […] The post Crystal Palace vs Aston Villa – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Glasner thriving at Crystal Palace after rollercoaster run of emotions
arts.sum: Manager marked first anniversary at club with victory and team are hoping to combine away record with more success at Selhurst Park Oliver Glasner comes across as one of the Premier League’s more measured managers, although sometimes he can’t help himself. When Daniel Muñoz scored Crystal Palace’s second goal at Craven Cottage on Saturday, the Austrian was one of the first to join the celebrations after sprinting down the touchline and was handed a yellow card for his efforts. It was the second time Glasner has been cautioned for the same offence after the Colombia defender’s late equaliser against Newcastle in November sparked similar scenes. “I try to be quite controlled but to be honest I don’t always find the midway point,” said Glasner, who marked the first anniversary of his appointment with a fourth straight Premier League away victory. “I like these emotions and I think it’s one of the main reasons that I like being a manager because you get all these emotions sometimes within a game. You can go from marriage to divorce three times in the same game … I hope that I will never have that in life – I’m married only once and I’m pretty sure and happy that it will last for my whole life!” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Oliver Glasner not looking beyond Aston Villa after celebrating a year at Crystal Palace
arts.sum: The current sequence of results came after Glasner and his side failed to win any of their opening eight top-flight fixtures – their worst-ever start to a Premier League campaign, itself coming off of an end-of-season run the previous campaign which saw the Eagles win six of their final seven games, scoring 21 goals, including the season-ending 5-0 victory against Aston Villa. After celebrating a year in the job last week, Crystal Palace boss Oliver Glasner is currently enjoying a run of form which has seen his side draw six and win seven of their last 17 Premier League games following their opening league win of the season against Spurs in late October.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace attacker to miss Aston Villa Premier League clash
arts.sum: With 60 minutes played for the U21s last week and a few minutes as a late substitute in the FA Cup win at Doncaster earlier this month, hopes were high that the 20-year-old would be back to full fitness soon, but a busy fixture period of three games in seven days means the opportunity for the young South American to train fully have been limited. Due to the Eagles’ clash with Newcastle United next month being postponed thanks to the league cup exploits of the north-east side, plus a scheduled international break, over three weeks separate the Ipswich game (March 8) to Palace’ next scheduled game, against Southampton (April 2) – although if the Eagles beat Millwall in the last 16 of the FA Cup this Saturday, they will move into the quarter-finals, scheduled for March 29.

arts.sum: Aston Villa head to Crystal Palace in the Premier League on Tuesday evening. Unai Emery’s side boosted their Champions League hopes with a 2-1 win over Chelsea at the weekend. Marco Asensio scored his first goals for the club with a brace, following his loan arrival from Paris Saint-Germain. Asensio’s double was assisted by Marcus […] The post Aston Villa XI vs Crystal Palace – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace to jet off to Marbella on mid-season training camp
arts.sum: Whatever the length of the break without a competitive game, the trip to Marbella is a carbon copy of the Eagles’ trip to Spain last year, where Glasner’s side faced Norwegian champions Bodo/Glimt in a friendly following the postponement of their clash against Newcastle due to their FA Cup progress. Due to the Eagles’ away clash with Newcastle United – originally scheduled for 15 March – being postponed to 16 April, thanks to the Carabao Cup exploits of the north-east side, plus a scheduled international break (17-25 March), over three weeks separate their Premier League game against Ipswich (8 March) to their next, scheduled game, against Southampton (2 April).
